inline_comments.message: These services no more needed, no need to inject them anymore.
NB: Can be formatted using the language support.
Did you forget to include the delete query here? It seems it is a custom query method since annotated by @Modifiying.
NB: The readability can be improved using language support and formatting by having the SQL inside three quotes """ """.
You can extract this into a constant variable with a meaningful query name.
NB: The readability can be improved using language support and formatting like in above query on line 71.
You can add javadoc for this interface.
I think no need this comment
Can be removed: there are lots of tests in *ModuleServiceIntegrationSpec*
Given the use of singletonList, these two methods could probably be refactored into one taking a Collection cmHandleIds.

I'd prefer conditional logging only when cmHandleIds.size > 0
Even better, return early if the input Map is empty.

Nb. I don't like that we use inheritance for this, it makes no sense - either use one single Persistence class, or use composition.

By inheritance, InventoryPersistence includes all the non-inventory related methods from NcmpPersistence. It would make more sense to use composition, i.e. InventoryPersistence uses NcmpPersistence.
Can be removed, given all tests passing.

BLOCKER!

In CPS REST API, we allow creating a SchemaSet as a single operation (without an anchor), so we should not delete orphaned ones ever!

It may make more sense to delete orphan Schema-Sets within a given dataspace, like *NFP-Operational* used by NCMP. Previous behaviour in CPS was that orphan *Yang modules* do get cleaned, but unused Schema-Sets do not.

I think a separate discussion & proposal for deletion is needed.
I don't mind this, because the method name is very descriptive.
Yeah. @Modifying should be removed here, since it's only needed if the interface extends JpaRepository, to auto-generate the implementation code from annotations.
I prefer inline constant for this TBH, it makes the code below easier to understand. I'd only bring a constant if it was used repeatedly.

Based on code analysis, I think *DuplicateYangResourceException* here means that the SchemaSet was **not** created. In that case, the handle should go to LOCKED state and be retried.

We should create a separate Jira to investigate this duplicate yang resource logic - I think the *createSchemaSetFromModules* method should internally resolve the conflict and continue, but it needs further analysis.

yeah, not 100% sure normally iteration should handle empty without extra code (-paths which needs coverage) But given the logging (the mislead me before) maybe its acceptable here..

On second look I think now i have the improved logging that clearly with show '0 cm handles' it is OK

i found these classnames a bit confusing to start with. going on the names 'Inventory' is PART of 'NCMP' an not the other way around. 
I'll see how I can improve it
I think this method is currently only called from (indirectly) testware but you are right, we need a discussion..
agree with Daniel extracting it wont add value

i think because the method is very small

will do
Agreed to create a new Jira ticket to investigate DuplicatedYangResourceException
Agreed to create Jira for adding CPS admin interface to clean unused schemaset/module data.
This method could be cleaned up now, since cascade delete is never used in any code path, i.e. cascadeDeleteAllowed is always CASCADE_DELETE_PROHIBITED now
ok, on 3rd look I saw there is quiet a bit of needless code and objects constructed if map is empty... So not just a simple iteration...
will do in separate commit (technical debt ticket even?!)
by putting it on next singe " sufficed...
there is a lot of good unit test around it, feels bad to remove it now if we for some reason want to introduce it again in other limited scenarios...
again not sure, maybe it should be left as  a valid potential usecase...  Lets discuss and in any case agree to handle at least in separate commit
this code is not covered by unit test (anymore?). Need to check if it was before refactorign and either way it should be covered

need to improve unit test
See the integration tests in YangModulesSpec.groovy tests these at the REST level, but only without module set tag.

Quotes are in the wrong place, leading to errors in integration tests. It should be:

    @Query(value = """
            DELETE FROM schema_set WHERE NOT EXISTS 
            (SELECT 1 FROM anchor WHERE anchor.schema_set_id = schema_set.id)
           """, nativeQuery = true)
NB: Not a good api doc, can be updated. first it is not a class but interface. second, not including the constant only but also method signatures.
NB: not a good variable name since it can be cmHandleId or moduleSetTag
NB: Redundant annotation & import not used in class
NB: comment closure not needed since it is mention on the slogan line 313
NB: no scenario exists in this test
NB: can be replaced by ''' ''' using language support. then concats are really ugly
Nb: can be replaced by ''' ''' using language support

This SQL is exactly the same as in SchemaSetRepository! It deletes unused schemasets... while it should delete orphaned yang resource references.
Though we need to double-check the DB schema for these tables to see what entries are automatically deleted, if any.
Added test case in YangModulesSpec using module set tag.
I added a new test for this called 'DMI Registration upgrade with exception while updating CM-handle state' in CmHandleRegistrationServiceSpec
Yes, SchemaSet name will be cmHandleId for legacy cases not using moduleSetTag. I'm not sure what else to call it other than 'schemaSetName'.
Liquibase rule 1-26 has cascade delete so that deleting SchemaSet will also delete SchemaSetYangResource entries. Thus this code is not needed.
Good approach getting it from a method. in this case, no name conflict occurs.
NB: Deletes multiple anchors identified by their IDs.
NB: Creates a CM handle and initiates the synchronization of modules to create a schema set and anchor.

I think it is OK to ignore this test during normal builds. It was mainly for investigation purposes.
Some off the asserts are very strict and can fail because of timing/ordering issues

Nb. Call it 'cmHandleIds' or 'anchorNames'

'anchorIds' may be confusing since in cps-ri, _anchorId_ refers to the entity ID in the DB, while _anchorName_ is a string like cmHandleId. In the context of NCMP _NFP-Operational_, anchor name is always cmHandleId.
